name: scenario-1-fix-works-offline
description: Offline twin of scenario-1: uses a hand-crafted GOOD patch (timeout_ms=200) so the demo's PASS case runs without ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Useful for CI and for offline kickoff demos. The non-offline scenario-1 lets Claude propose the patch.

alert.title: [p99 high] checkout latency above SLO
alert.event_msg: Monitor 'checkout.p99.latency' triggered. p99 is 1,420ms; SLO is 500ms.
alert.body: Monitor 'checkout.p99.latency' triggered. p99 is 1,420ms; SLO is 500ms.

prepared_patch.summary: Wrap fetch_price in a 25ms timeout. The client already supports the kwarg.
prepared_patch.hypothesis: The pricing service has a long latency tail. Without a timeout, checkout p99 inherits it.
repro_steps: Observe p99 latency before and after the patch

files.before:
"""Checkout endpoint.

PLANTED BUG: `fetch_price` is called with no client-side timeout. The pricing
service has a 10% tail-latency profile (~1.5s per affected call), so any
request that lands on a tail call inherits the full 1.5s wait. With even
modest traffic, p99 latency drifts well above SLO and the alert fires.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from .pricing import fetch_price


def checkout(item_id: str, qty: int) -> dict:
    # BUG: no timeout on the downstream pricing call.
    price = fetch_price(item_id)
    return {"item_id": item_id, "qty": qty, "total": price * qty}
